theme: Which side of the 9:30 candle gets hit FIRST
method: Subject = 9:30-9:45 candle. After it closes, scan forward 15m bars; record which side (H or L) gets wicked first. Both same bar = ambiguous.
key_finding: First-touch direction is essentially a coin flip — high vs low first is 48/47, almost perfectly balanced. The previously observed 'high-bias' in the cumulative retest stats is thus driven by SECOND-touch dynamics (after a sweep), not by directional asymmetry of the first move.
